observations: SCOPE-DRIFT CHECK (programmatic, byte-level diff against round 0, reconstructed from this evaluator's own round-0 Read): 'scope.files_create' is empty in both rounds; 'scope.files_modify' differs only in the tools/validate.sh purpose string gaining the disclosed phrase 'including a per-family minimum-coverage dormancy guard'. non_goals grew 17 -> 18: exactly ONE bullet is genuinely new (the classification-regex-residual-risk candidate-NDEBT non-goal); the pre-existing --target-routing-collision non-goal and the docs/planning non-goal were only lightly reworded (adding 'and independently re-confirmed byte-identical by the evaluator's round-0 review' and pluralizing 'candidate NDEBT entries' respectively) -- both rewordings are factually accurate, not scope-inflating. design_notes gained exactly three new keys (dormancy_guard_fix_rev1, negative_marker_design_and_ndebt_014_rev1 replacing the round-0 negative_marker_design_and_ndebt_014, classification_regex_residual_risk_ndebt_candidate); mechanism_chosen, why_no_new_ci_workflow_file, and no_new_fixtures_needed are BYTE-IDENTICAL to round 0 (confirmed by direct string comparison, not visual inspection). No unrelated scope drift found anywhere in the contract.

VERIFICATION-ARRAY DIFF (byte-level, all 19 entries re-executed verbatim in a disposable clone, extracted from the contract JSON via json.load to avoid transcription risk -- 19/19 PASS): entries 1-4 are byte-identical to round 0's entries 1-4 (confirmed via direct string comparison of both 'acceptance_test' and 'command'), CONTRADICTING the contract's own approvals.round0_disposition claim that 'entry 1's anti-vacuity check' was 'renumbered/simplified for clarity' -- entry 1 is unchanged, not renumbered or simplified. This is a minor, non-blocking self-description inaccuracy in the contract's own bookkeeping narrative (not a functional defect in the verification suite itself, and not a repeat of the 041 content-honesty class since no acceptance test's own behavior is misrepresented -- only a prose summary of the diff is imprecise). Entries 13-19 are byte-identical to round 0's entries 9-15. Entry 12 (the rewritten NDEBT-014 guard) is confirmably DIFFERENT from round 0's entry 8, as intended. Entries 5, 7, and 9 (the three combinations preserved from round 0's entries 5/6/7) are functionally unchanged -- diffed and found to differ ONLY in their failure-message string (e.g. 'load-bearing proof (baseline family) failed' -> 'load-bearing proof (baseline, negative-made-valid) failed'), never in the mutation or assertion logic. Entries 6, 8, and 10 are the three genuinely new combinations. Entry 11 (dormancy guard) is wholly new. Net: 15 + 3 (new combinations) + 1 (dormancy guard) = 19, exactly matching the array's measured growth -- the count is fully explained without needing entry 1 to have changed, corroborating that the 'entry 1 renumbered' claim is spurious.

HIGH FINDING #1 (dormancy vacuity) RE-VERIFIED CLOSED, WITH TWO ADDITIONAL FRESH PROBES BEYOND THE CONTRACT'S OWN ENTRY 11: built an independent rev1 prototype of check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures (per design_notes.dormancy_guard_fix_rev1 -- per-family positive/negative list computed before validation, each family independently asserting >=1 of each polarity) in a disposable clone of real HEAD d99d57a. Re-ran entry 11's literal script (all three families) verbatim: PASS, confirming C12 FAILs naming both 'zero POSITIVE fixtures' and 'zero NEGATIVE fixtures' for whichever family's fixtures were relocated, sweep exit non-zero, in ~43s total across 3 disposable sub-clones. FRESH PROBE A (single-polarity dormancy, not in the contract's own entry 11): moved ONLY the negative fixtures for ecosystem_baseline out of tools/fixtures/, leaving the positive fixture in place -- C12 correctly FAILED naming ONLY 'ecosystem_baseline: zero NEGATIVE fixtures ... (NDEBT-009 regression)' (not a false 'zero POSITIVE' claim), confirming the guard is precisely per-polarity, not merely per-family. FRESH PROBE B (total-empty tools/fixtures/ directory): removed every file from tools/fixtures/ entirely (not just the three families' files) in a disposable clone -- C12 correctly FAILED, naming all six lines (zero POSITIVE and zero NEGATIVE for each of the three families independently), sweep exit non-zero. Both probes confirm the dormancy guard is robust beyond the contract's own tested scenario (per-family, both-polarities-at-once relocation) to the two adjacent failure shapes (single-polarity loss, and total fixture-directory evacuation).

HIGH FINDING #2 (proxy test) RE-VERIFIED CLOSED, WITH TWO INDEPENDENTLY-CONSTRUCTED MUTANTS BEYOND THE CONTRACT'S OWN CLAIM: re-ran entry 12's literal extraction-based script against the rev1 prototype's honest, shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex (`re.compile(r"(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)")`) -- PASS. Built my OWN first mutant (not the contract's own claimed mutant, an independently-authored one): in a fresh disposable clone, sed-replaced the shipped pattern with a naive, buggy `re.compile(r"invalid")` (dropping the 'neg' alternative entirely) -- entry 12, re-run verbatim against this mutant, correctly printed 'the shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern misclassified a case' and exited 1. This independently corroborates the contract's own claimed mutant-based proof rather than merely re-trusting it. Built a SECOND, more probing mutant not claimed anywhere in the contract: a semantically-IDENTICAL refactor that extracts the same pattern string to a named constant (`NEG_PATTERN_STR = "..."` then `NEGATIVE_TOKEN = re.compile(NEG_PATTERN_STR)`) -- functionally correct code (confirmed: the full sweep still runs green, 12 passed 0 failed, C12's actual classification behavior is unaffected) -- yet entry 12 correctly printed 'COULD NOT EXTRACT the shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern ... classification logic may have been refactored without updating this test' and exited 1. This is INTENTIONAL, DISCLOSED, conservative fail-closed behavior (design_notes.negative_marker_design_and_ndebt_014_rev1 explicitly states entry 12 'fails loudly if the pattern cannot be extracted at all'), not a hidden defect -- flagged here as a residual (non-blocking) brittleness: entry 12 is now coupled to the EXACT literal source form `NEGATIVE_TOKEN = re.compile(r"...")`, so a future benign refactor (renaming, reformatting, or extracting the pattern to a constant) would require updating entry 12's extraction regex in lockstep, even though nothing would actually be broken. The direction of this brittleness (blocks CI loudly, never silently passes) is the safe one for a meta-check of this kind, and is explicitly disclosed in the contract's own design_notes, so it is not treated as a blocking finding.

MEDIUM FINDING #3 (6-way coverage) RE-VERIFIED CLOSED: literal re-execution of entries 5-10 against the rev1 prototype confirms all six (family x direction) combinations independently flip C12 to FAIL: ecosystem_baseline negative-made-valid and positive-broken; engineering_finding positive-broken and negative-made-valid; preflight_verdict negative-made-valid and positive-broken. All six PASS (i.e. the acceptance test that C12 correctly FAILs under each mutation succeeds).

CLASSIFICATION-REGEX RESIDUAL RISK CORRECTLY CARRIED FORWARD, NOT RE-OPENED: confirmed design_notes.classification_regex_residual_risk_ndebt_candidate and the corresponding new non_goals bullet are present, verbatim disclosing the same case-sensitivity/full-word dodges and the '..._invalid_handling.json' false-trigger this evaluator's round-0 review surfaced, correctly framed as a non-blocking candidate NDEBT entry rather than fixed in-contract -- consistent with this evaluator's own round-0 recommendation and the contract's own precedent of flagging-not-fixing the adjacent --target routing collision.

NDEBT-014 BRACKET-BUG SWEEP (all 19 entries, literal re-execution): all 19 of the contract's own verification 'command' strings were extracted verbatim via json.load and executed unmodified against the rev1 prototype clone; all 19 print PASS (entry 11's three-family loop took ~43s, entries 5-10's six mktemp-clone probes took roughly 10-15s each -- full 19-entry re-run comfortably fits within a single evaluation pass but benefits from a >60s per-command allowance given the mktemp+cp-a+full-sweep pattern repeated up to 3x within a single entry). No quoting, heredoc, or bracket defect found.

SIBLING-CHECK NON-INTERFERENCE CONFIRMED: throughout every dormancy/relocation probe (single-family, single-polarity, and total-empty-directory), C1 through C11 continued to print PASS unaffected -- the dormancy guard's failure signal is confined to C12's own output line and the overall SUMMARY count, never bleeding into or masking an unrelated check.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` on the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was checked before and after this entire round-2 review and shows only the three pre-existing tolerated/expected untracked entries (.agent/contracts/042.json, .agent/qa/042-contract-review.json from round 0, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) throughout, plus this review's own new artifact. All prototype construction, both fresh dormancy probes, and both independently-authored regex mutants ran only inside disposable `mktemp -d`-rooted clones under the evaluator's session scratchpad; no real repository path was created, edited, or deleted by this review.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1, fit for implementation as-is. Both round-0 HIGH findings are genuinely and robustly closed, verified with independent probes beyond what the contract itself claims, not merely by re-trusting its narrative. HIGH #1 (dormancy vacuity): re-ran entry 11 verbatim (PASS, all three families) and additionally constructed two fresh probes of my own -- single-polarity dormancy (moving only a family's negative fixtures, positive left in place) correctly fails naming only the missing polarity, and total-empty tools/fixtures/ (every file removed) correctly fails naming all six family/polarity lines -- confirming the guard is precise and does not falsely blame the wrong polarity. HIGH #2 (proxy test): re-ran entry 12 verbatim against the honest shipped regex (PASS), then independently built two of my own mutants (not the contract's own claimed mutant) -- a naive buggy regex missing the 'neg' alternative is correctly caught, and a semantically-identical refactor extracting the pattern to a named constant is correctly rejected with an explicit 'COULD NOT EXTRACT' message, confirming entry 12 is now genuinely bound to the shipped source bytes and fails safely (never silently passes) even under a benign refactor, an intentional and disclosed conservative tradeoff, not a hidden defect. The MEDIUM finding (6-way coverage) is closed: all six family x direction combinations (entries 5-10) independently re-executed and pass. A systematic byte-level diff against a reconstruction of the round-0 contract confirms no undisclosed scope drift: files_create/files_modify unchanged modulo the disclosed dormancy-guard wording; non_goals grew by exactly one genuinely-new bullet (classification-regex residual risk), with two other bullets only lightly and accurately reworded; entries 1-4 and 13-19 are byte-identical to round 0; the three preserved load-bearing combinations (now entries 5, 7, 9) differ from round 0 only in their failure-message string, never their mutation/assertion logic. One minor, non-blocking observation: the contract's own approvals.round0_disposition prose claims entry 1 was 'renumbered/simplified for clarity', which this review's byte-diff shows to be inaccurate (entry 1 is unchanged) -- a cosmetic self-description slip in the contract's own narrative, not a defect in the verification suite's actual behavior, and not blocking for implementation. The classification-regex residual-risk observation from round 0 is correctly carried forward as a disclosed, non-blocking candidate-NDEBT limitation rather than fixed, consistent with this evaluator's own round-0 recommendation. Fit for implementation as-is.
